cover.title: How-to, how-to.
cover.sub: build a polished walkthrough PDF — two ways.
cover.intro: Turn screenshots or plain content into a clean, uniform guide PDF: a typeset cover, every image on a shadowed white card with a hairline border, optional numbered step headers, UI patch-outs, and an optional slide counter. One consistent look — two flows.

index.d: Pick by whether you have images.

slides.title: Two flows
slides.lead: pick by whether you already have images.

blocks.label: Which flow
blocks.html: Images provided (screenshots, exported diagrams) → Flow 1: assemble them directly. No images (documenting a concept, an API, a checklist — nothing to screenshot) → Flow 2: generate styled slides from content, then assemble them.

blocks.label: Setup
blocks.html: Both flows are Python. Assembling needs Pillow; generating (Flow 2) also needs headless Chrome. The skill uses progressive disclosure — SKILL.md is a lean router that points you at references/flow-images.md or references/flow-content.md for the full steps.

blocks.label: What you get
blocks.html: Each image centered on a shadowed white card with a 1px border; an optional typeset cover (eyebrow + title + intro); optional numbered step headers on result slides; patch-outs for logos or controls (solid fill or gradient-safe column copy); an optional footer slide counter.

blocks.label: How to run
blocks.html: Write a guide.json listing your images (with optional per-slide crop / patches / header) and run build_guide_pdf.py --config guide.json. For a plain folder, quick mode: --images ./shots --output Guide.pdf. Full detail in references/flow-images.md.

slides.title: Flow 2 — generate
slides.lead: no screenshots? render them from content.

blocks.label: What it does
blocks.html: Turns a content config into styled HTML slides → PNGs via headless Chrome, in the design aesthetic (warm paper, one rust accent, serif display + mono labels, hairline rules, corner ticks). Then hands those PNGs to Flow 1's assembler — so you still get the same cover, cards, and framing.

blocks.label: How to run
blocks.html: Write a slides.json (a cover + one entry per topic) and run render_slides.py --config slides.json; assemble the PNGs with build_guide_pdf.py using "footer": false (the generated slides carry their own footer). Full detail in references/flow-content.md. (This guide was built with Flow 2.)
